pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the Zod repository at a high level, explaining what Zod is, its purpose as a TypeScript-first schema validation library, and the overall structure of the repository

pages.title: Repository Structure
pages.purpose: Provide a comprehensive overview of the monorepo’s structure and organization. Document all packages (e.g., zod, docs, bench, core, examples, tests) with clear explanations of their roles, dependencies, and interactions. Describe how the workspace is organized, including directory layout, build tooling, dependency management, and shared configurations. Explain naming conventions, how packages are versioned and published, and how local development, testing, and CI/CD workflows operate within the monorepo. Include guidelines for adding new packages, maintaining consistency, and scaling the repository as it grows.

pages.title: Core Concepts
pages.purpose: Explain fundamental Zod concepts: schemas, validation, parsing, type inference, and the relationship between runtime validation and compile-time types

pages.title: Core Library Architecture
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the Zod library's internal architecture, explaining the multi-layer design (Core, Classic, Mini), schema type hierarchy, and validation engine

pages.title: Schema Type System
pages.purpose: Document the schema type hierarchy, including $ZodType base class, primitive types, complex types, and how schemas are constructed and initialized

pages.title: Validation Pipeline
pages.purpose: Explain the validation flow from input through parse/safeParse methods, including synchronous vs asynchronous validation, check execution, and result generation

pages.title: Type Inference System
pages.purpose: Document how Zod extracts TypeScript types from schemas using z.infer, z.input, z.output, and how this achieves compile-time type safety

pages.title: Schema Definitions
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to defining schemas, covering all schema types (string, number, object, array, union, etc.) and their APIs

pages.title: Primitive Schemas
pages.purpose: Document string, number, boolean, bigint, date, and other primitive schema types, including their validation methods and format checks

pages.title: Complex Schemas
pages.purpose: Document object, array, tuple, union, intersection, and other complex schema types, including their composition patterns

pages.title: Validation Checks
pages.purpose: Explain the check system, including built-in checks (min, max, regex), format checks (email, UUID, URL), and custom refinements

pages.title: Schema Wrappers
pages.purpose: Document wrapper schemas that modify base schemas: optional, nullable, default, catch, transform, pipe, readonly

pages.title: Error Handling
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to error handling in Zod, covering ZodError structure, error customization, internationalization, and error precedence

pages.title: ZodError Structure
pages.purpose: Document the ZodError class, issues array, error codes, and how validation failures are reported with detailed context

pages.title: Error Customization
pages.purpose: Explain how to customize error messages at different levels (schema, parse, global, locale) and the precedence hierarchy

pages.title: Internationalization
pages.purpose: Document the locale system with 40+ language support, how to use locale error maps, and deprecated locale aliases

pages.title: Advanced Features
pages.purpose: Cover advanced Zod features including codecs, JSON Schema conversion, and metadata system

pages.title: Codec System
pages.purpose: Explain the codec system for bidirectional transformations, including encode/decode operations, refinement chains, and type safety

pages.title: JSON Schema Conversion
pages.purpose: Document how to convert Zod schemas to JSON Schema format, supporting Draft 2020-12, Draft 7, and OpenAPI 3.0

pages.title: Metadata and Registries
pages.purpose: Explain the schema registry system for associating metadata with schemas, including the global registry and custom registries

pages.title: API Variants
pages.purpose: Document the different API surfaces Zod provides: Classic (full-featured), Mini (tree-shakable), and version differences (v3 vs v4)

pages.title: Classic API
pages.purpose: Document the Classic API (default) with its full feature set, method chaining patterns, and compatibility with v3

pages.title: Mini API
pages.purpose: Explain the Mini API variant designed for tree-shaking and reduced bundle sizes, including its functional approach and differences from Classic

pages.title: Version Migration
pages.purpose: Guide for migrating from Zod v3 to v4, covering breaking changes, new features, performance improvements, and versioning strategy

pages.title: Build and Distribution
pages.purpose: Document the build system, compilation process, output formats, and distribution to NPM, JSR, and Deno

pages.title: Build System
pages.purpose: Explain the build pipeline using pnpm, tsdown compiler, stub package.json generation, and multi-format output (CJS, ESM, types)

pages.title: Package Structure
pages.purpose: Document the package.json configuration, module exports architecture (9 entry points), and how different formats are resolved

pages.title: CI/CD Pipeline
pages.purpose: Explain the GitHub Actions workflows for testing, releasing (regular and canary), and deployment to multiple registries

pages.title: Testing and Benchmarking
pages.purpose: Document the test suite, benchmarking system for performance comparison, and quality assurance processes

pages.title: Test Suite
pages.purpose: Document the Vitest-based test suite, test organization, TypeScript version matrix testing, and coverage

pages.title: Benchmark Suite
pages.purpose: Explain the benchmarking system comparing v3, v4, arktype, and valibot, including performance metrics and test scenarios

pages.title: Ecosystem
pages.purpose: Overview of the Zod ecosystem including 30+ integrations, API libraries, form libraries, converters, and developer tools

pages.title: Integration Categories
pages.purpose: Document the seven integration categories: API libraries, form integrations, converters (Zod-to-X and X-to-Zod), mocking, projects, and utilities

pages.title: Documentation Site
pages.purpose: Explain how the documentation site is built, including MDX content, component structure, and dynamic features like star fetching

pages.title: Contributing
pages.purpose: Guide for contributors covering development setup, code quality standards, testing requirements, and contribution workflow

pages.title: Development Setup
pages.purpose: Instructions for setting up the development environment, including pnpm installation, dev containers, and the play.ts workflow

pages.title: Code Quality
pages.purpose: Document code quality tools and processes: pre-commit hooks (husky + lint-staged), biome linting/formatting, and TypeScript checks
